assertion_results.text: Output file AGENTS.md exists in project root
Contains ## Overview section with project description
assertion_results.evidence: No ## Overview section. Has ## File Map and ## Codebase State sections that contain project context, but no dedicated Overview heading with a project description paragraph.

assertion_results.text: Contains a commands or build section with make targets
assertion_results.evidence: ## Commands (verified) section with table listing 14 make targets including vet, fmt-check, lint, test, build, docker-build, setup, dev-check, security-check, mutation-test

assertion_results.text: Listed make targets match actual Makefile targets
assertion_results.evidence: All 14 listed targets (vet, fmt-check, fmt, lint, test, test-race, test-integration, test-smoke, build, docker-build, setup, dev-check, security-check, mutation-test) verified to exist in Makefile. Also includes 'go test -v ./core/...' which is valid though not a Makefile target.
Codebase State section: 'Go 1.26.1 (`go.mod`)' -- matches actual go.mod content 'go 1.26.1'

assertion_results.text: File paths mentioned in AGENTS.md reference files that actually exist (spot-check 5 paths)
assertion_results.evidence: All 18 directory paths in File Map verified (ofelia.go, cli/, core/, core/adapters/, core/domain/, core/ports/, config/, middlewares/, etc.). All 16 Golden Samples + Utilities paths verified (core/runjob.go, core/resilient_job.go, core/adapters/docker/client.go, etc.). 0 missing.

assertion_results.text: Uses tables or structured format (not just prose paragraphs)
assertion_results.evidence: Uses markdown tables extensively: Commands table (14 rows), Golden Samples table (7 rows), Utilities table (9 rows), Heuristics table (8 rows), Terminology table (8 rows). Also uses code blocks for File Map.

assertion_results.text: Does not duplicate content from README.md
assertion_results.evidence: README.md covers user-facing documentation (badges, features, usage, environment variables, configuration). AGENTS.md covers agent-specific concerns (commands, file map, golden samples, utilities, heuristics, boundaries). No content overlap detected.

qualitative.structure_quality: Excellent. Well-organized with AGENTS-GENERATED markers, clear section hierarchy, consistent formatting. Includes workflow, heuristics, boundaries (always/ask/never), terminology, and scoped AGENTS.md index.
qualitative.conciseness: Good. 189 lines is reasonable for a complex Go project with ~30 Makefile targets. Information is dense and actionable, not padded.
qualitative.accuracy: Excellent. All file paths, make targets, Go version, and dependency references verified against actual project. Go 1.26.1 matches go.mod.
qualitative.usefulness_for_agents: Excellent. Golden Samples provide pattern references. Utilities prevent reinvention. Heuristics give quick decision trees. Boundaries are clear. JSON serialization note is a real gotcha that would save agent time.
